mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% in June 2013 showing downtrend from 2012 peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic optimism, which increases multiplier effects through employment and materials consumption, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 12-month moving average by at least 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 914k units in May 2013, recovering from 2012 lows
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) when HOUST exceeds trigger threshold, or correlation between HOUST growth and subsequent 60d returns falls below 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPI) begins to accelerate, real rates compress further, reducing the discount rate applied to long-duration equity cash flows and producing negative forward returns as investors anticipate eventual tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPI at 231.8 in mid-2013
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL interaction term has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints, which compress profit margins and reduce forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 in May 2013 showing modest growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5d forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating payroll employment signals robust aggregate demand and consumer purchasing power, which drives earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both signals align.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 422.8B and PAYEMS at 135.9M in June 2013
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS growth has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth exceeding 200k jobs per month signals tight labor market conditions and wage pressure, which increases corporate labor costs and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as margin compression becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 200k jobs per month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135.9M in June 2013 with steady monthly gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth rate and subsequent 60d returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) remain deeply negative, which incentivizes equity risk-taking and portfolio reallocation away from cash, producing sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as long as this condition persists.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in mid-2013 with FEDFUNDS near 0% and CPI inflation ~2%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with financial repression
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of all observations when real rates are negative, or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate revenue growth, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13725.7B in Q1 2013 showing moderate growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60d forward returns is below 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.
